content: agent2 here: implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options (dict-based defaults like default={'--protocol': {'http': 80}}). Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, Command.main()) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will coordinate before major structural changes.

agent2: my dict-default changes in core.py: added a module-level _validate_dynamic_default() helper near _check_iter (~line 1984), Option.__init__ intercepts dict defaults right at the top (before the default_is_missing computation / super().__init__ call, ~line 2554-2568) storing self._dynamic_default and zeroing attrs['default'], Option.get_help_extra() dynamic-default branch (~line 2887-2896), and a new Command.parse_args()+Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() second pass (~line 1189-1260). No type_cast_value override, no to_info_dict changes. My Option.__init__ edit is right at the top of the method body, ahead of your batch_size additions further down — should be an easy textual merge, but flag if your batch_size edits also touch the very first lines of __init__ (the default_is_missing / super().__init__ call).
